title: Reading a peptide, letter by letter
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: This is how a mass spectrometer spells out a protein. Smash the peptide PEPTIDE apart at its backbone bonds and every break leaves two charged pieces — a b-ion counted from the front, a y-ion counted from the back. Each peak on the spectrum is one such fragment, and the GAP between neighbouring peaks in a series is the mass of a single amino acid: step along the b-ions and you literally read P-E-P-T-I-D-E off the spacings. It is the trick behind shotgun proteomics, where software sequences thousands of peptides a second from nothing but these mass ladders. Deterministic monoisotopic masses — the same peptide always yields the same rungs.
